original_image_tags: A single page of a typewritten document on dark, textured paper, possibly carbon paper. Two hole-punch circles are visible at the top. The text is light-colored against the dark background. The word "COPY" is stamped diagonally in the upper right corner. The word "CONFIDENTIAL" is stamped at the top and bottom center.

elements.description: A declassified confidential typed document dated 18 August 1947. It contains a telephone message from Mr. Al Reck of the Oakland Tribune regarding an incident reported by Mr. Ray A. Switzer on August 14 near Placerville, California. The text describes a 'rocket-shaped' object crashing with a 'puff of black smoke'. The page features 'CONFIDENTIAL' stamps at the top and bottom, a 'COPY' stamp at the top right, and two punch holes at the top.

judge.reason: Both agree on the same visual elements: two punch holes, 'CONFIDENTIAL' stamps at top and bottom, and 'COPY' stamp at top right; Description B adds typed text content that Description A omits but does not contradict A.
